key_insight: Vitriflow makes melt–quench amorphous-structure generation an explicit, calibrated decision chain instead of a set of hidden simulation choices.
engineering_relevance: It helps materials engineers generate reproducible amorphous ensembles with protocol settings and screening criteria tailored to the target material and analysis goal.
method: Calibrate numerical stability and melt/quench parameters in a descriptor space.
Apply user-defined artefact screening to remove defective or recrystallised structures.
Check statistical convergence of the final analysis ensemble for the material of interest.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations on a-SiO2, a-Si3N4, and a-Sm2O3, showing defect separation, DFT-refinement response tracking, and recrystallisation filtering across distinct materials.
watch_item: Calibration and screening are material-specific, so the workflow still depends on careful descriptor choice and may need validation when transferred to new chemistries.
paper_title: Vitriflow: calibrated amorphous structure ensembles from melt-quench simulation
